payload.source_text: The pocket money tracker system allows children to claim pocket money tasks as they are completed throughout the week.
Parents can detail tasks that should be completed and the monetary amount earned for that task.
As a task is completed it is linked to the child that completed it.
The children should be able to see the available tasks and the tasks they have completed that week.
At the end of each week the children can see the total amount they have earned that week.
The Parents can also see a report of how much each child has earned and the tasks they completed so that they can provide the pocket money earned to each child.
At the start of the next week all tasks become available again to be claimed.
Historical task data shall be available.
The resulting system shall be able to operate on iOS 16, Android 9 and Windows 11 or later.

payload.completeness_rule: This register SHALL contain the identifiers of ALL ZachmanCell elements present in the ledger.

payload.description: Task as a unit of work assigned to children
payload.justification: Task is referenced across multiple signals as a strategically important thing that drives the pocket money system.

payload.description: Pocket money as monetary reward earned by children
payload.justification: Pocket money is the central entity of strategic importance, representing the financial value exchanged in the system.

payload.description: Earnings record of child's accumulated rewards
payload.justification: Earnings are implied as a strategically important data entity that tracks weekly accumulated value for children.

payload.description: Weekly earnings reporting and payment cycle
payload.justification: Weekly cycle is implied by references to 'weekly earnings' and periodic pocket money distribution, representing a strategically significant temporal pattern.

payload.description: Child user claiming pocket money and completing tasks
payload.justification: Children are identified as a primary stakeholder actor who claims rewards, views tasks, and tracks earnings.

payload.description: Parent user defining tasks and managing rewards
payload.justification: Parents are identified as a primary stakeholder actor responsible for task creation, reward assignment, and payment distribution.

payload.description: Enable children to claim pocket money through task completion
payload.justification: SG915 explicitly states this as the system's primary motivational goal and core purpose.

payload.description: Enable parental control over task definition and monetary reward assignment
payload.justification: SG916 describes parental control motivation as a key purpose, establishing strategic intent for parent-driven task and reward management.

payload.description: Provide children with visibility into available and completed tasks
payload.justification: SG917 expresses child transparency as a motivational goal focused on task visibility at strategic level.

payload.description: Enable children to understand their weekly earnings through reward visibility
payload.justification: SG918 articulates accountability and reward visibility as a strategic purpose for earnings transparency.

payload.description: Enable parental oversight of task completion and earnings
payload.justification: SG919 describes parental oversight as a strategic goal, providing visibility to support pocket money decisions.

payload.description: Facilitate parental payment distribution based on task completion
payload.justification: SG919 identifies payment facilitation as a distinct strategic goal supporting the pocket money distribution process.

payload.description: Task claiming process enabling children to claim pocket money upon task completion
payload.justification: Core business capability described as fundamental process for claiming pocket money

payload.description: Task viewing function providing children visibility into available and completed tasks
payload.justification: Visibility capability requirement describes a distinct viewing function for child actors

payload.description: Earnings reporting function aggregating child task completion and payment data for parents
payload.justification: Parent-facing reporting capability described as distinct aggregation function

payload.description: Weekly task lifecycle rule governing task availability reset behavior
payload.justification: Normative signal defines temporal business rule for weekly task availability and reset

payload.description: Historical task retention rule ensuring persistence beyond current operational period
payload.justification: Data retention requirement expressed as normative business rule for task entity lifecycle

payload.description: Task business entity representing work items available for completion
payload.justification: Multiple signals describe tasks as core business entities with monetary value, creation by parents, and visibility requirements

payload.description: Child business entity representing users who complete tasks and earn money
payload.justification: Child is clearly identified as a business entity with relationships to tasks and earnings aggregations

payload.description: Parent business entity representing users who create tasks and make payments
payload.justification: Parent identified as business entity with task creation and payment reporting capabilities

payload.description: Monetary amount attribute defining the value associated with each task
payload.justification: Monetary amount is explicitly described as defining attribute in task creation capability

payload.description: Task completion status attribute distinguishing available from completed tasks
payload.justification: Visibility requirement implies completion status as a distinguishing attribute of task entities

payload.description: Weekly earnings aggregate entity representing total child income for a given period
payload.justification: Weekly aggregation is described as a derived business entity providing visibility into total earnings

payload.description: Task creation relationship between parent entities and task entities
payload.justification: Explicit relationship defining parent capability to create task entities with monetary amounts

payload.description: Task completion relationship associating child entities with completed task entities
payload.justification: Normative signal establishes conceptual relationship between completed tasks and child entities

payload.description: Weekly task availability cycle defining periodic reset of task inventory
payload.justification: Temporal rule explicitly defines weekly cycle governing task availability lifecycle

payload.description: Task completion event marking the moment a child completes a task
payload.justification: Task completion is a significant business event enabling claiming process and establishing child-task relationship

payload.description: Child role responsible for viewing and completing tasks to earn money
payload.justification: Child identified as distinct business role with specific capabilities for task completion and earnings visibility

payload.description: Parent role responsible for creating tasks and reviewing child earnings for payment
payload.justification: Parent identified as distinct business role with task creation and payment reporting responsibilities

payload.description: Enable children to earn pocket money through task completion
payload.justification: Fundamental business capability describes core goal of the pocket money system

payload.description: Provide children visibility into their earning opportunities and accomplishments
payload.justification: Visibility capabilities serve the business goal of transparency for child users

payload.description: Enable parents to track child earnings and task completion for payment decisions
payload.justification: Parent reporting capability serves the goal of informed payment management

payload.description: Task availability must reset on a weekly cycle
payload.justification: Temporal business rule establishes mandatory weekly reset constraint on task availability

payload.description: Historical task data must be retained beyond current operational period
payload.justification: Normative requirement establishes mandatory constraint on task entity persistence

payload.completeness_rule: This register SHALL contain the identifiers of ALL CellContentItem elements present in the ledger.

payload.name: Household Participation
payload.description: Defines who participates in the household economic system and their fundamental roles, rights, and accountabilities within the family structure.

payload.name: Work Obligation
payload.description: Establishes the enterprise commitment to defining, assigning, and tracking units of work required for economic participation and reward eligibility.

payload.name: Economic Reward Stewardship
payload.description: Governs the enterprise's accountability for monetary reward determination, accumulation, transparency, and distribution to participating members.

payload.name: Parental Oversight
payload.description: Defines the enterprise commitment to oversight, validation, and governance of work completion and economic outcomes within the household.

payload.name: Task Stewardship
payload.description: Business responsibility for establishing and governing the inventory of work items that constitute earning opportunities, including their monetary values, lifecycle states, and periodic renewal obligations.

payload.name: Task Fulfillment Accountability
payload.description: Business responsibility for governing the completion of work items by authorized participants, including the claiming of associated monetary entitlements and the determination of completion status.

payload.name: Earnings Record Stewardship
payload.description: Business responsibility for maintaining authoritative records of income entitlements derived from completed work, aggregated across defined periods, and retained for accountability and payment decision support.

payload.name: Participant Transparency Obligation
payload.description: Business responsibility for ensuring participants have appropriate visibility into their earning opportunities, work accomplishments, and income entitlements to support informed participation and accountability.

payload.completeness_rule: This register SHALL contain the identifiers of ALL Domain elements present in the ledger.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall recognise child participants who claim pocket money and complete tasks.
payload.rationale: Establishes the structural composition of the enterprise by defining one category of participant (child) and their fundamental relationship to the household economic system.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall recognise parent participants who define tasks and manage rewards.
payload.rationale: Establishes the structural composition of the enterprise by defining a second category of participant (parent) and their fundamental relationship to the household economic system.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall establish a unit of work assigned to children.
payload.rationale: Defines the fundamental structural entity (Task) that the enterprise recognises as the basis for work obligation and economic participation.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall enable parental authority to define work obligations and assign monetary rewards.
payload.rationale: Commits the enterprise to providing parents with control over task definition and reward assignment, reflecting the governance model of the household economic system.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall provide children with visibility into available and completed work obligations.
payload.rationale: Establishes the enterprise commitment to transparency, enabling children to see both opportunities for economic participation and their historical performance.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall provide monetary reward to children for task completion.
payload.rationale: Establishes the enterprise's commitment to deliver pocket money as a reward mechanism, derived from the What-entity (pocket money as monetary reward) and the Why-goal (enable claiming through task completion).

payload.statement: The enterprise shall maintain earnings records for each child.
payload.rationale: Expresses the enterprise's accountability for maintaining accumulated reward records, derived from the What-entity describing earnings records.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall report and disburse earnings on a weekly cycle.
payload.rationale: Commits the enterprise to a weekly temporal obligation for both reporting and payment activities, derived from the When-cycle CCI.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall provide visibility of weekly earnings to children.
payload.rationale: Establishes the enterprise's obligation to enable understanding through reward visibility, derived from the Why-goal of enabling children to understand their weekly earnings.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall establish oversight authority for household task completion and economic outcomes.
payload.rationale: Defines the enterprise governance commitment to parental oversight as a structural authority over task validation and economic management within the household scope.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall enable validation of task completion outcomes.
payload.rationale: Expresses the enterprise capability to provide mechanisms for oversight of task completion status, derived from the parental oversight goal.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall enable distribution of economic compensation based on validated task completion.
payload.rationale: Captures the enterprise capability to facilitate payment distribution contingent upon completion validation, maintaining the causal relationship between task completion and economic outcome.

payload.statement: The business shall maintain a record of tasks representing work items available for completion.
payload.rationale: Establishes the business responsibility to maintain the core task inventory that constitutes earning opportunities.

payload.statement: Each task shall have a monetary amount defining its value.
payload.rationale: Defines the structural requirement that tasks carry a value attribute essential to earning opportunity assessment.

payload.statement: Each task shall have a completion status distinguishing available tasks from completed tasks.
payload.rationale: Establishes the structural obligation for tasks to maintain lifecycle state necessary for business governance.

payload.statement: Tasks shall be created through a relationship with parent entities.
payload.rationale: Defines the structural relationship governing task provenance and ownership within the business model.

payload.statement: The parent shall be responsible for creating tasks and reviewing child earnings for payment.
payload.rationale: Establishes the business role accountability for task creation and payment review activities.

payload.statement: The business shall enforce task availability reset on a weekly cycle.
payload.rationale: Expresses the business governance rule requiring periodic renewal of task inventory, combining the lifecycle rule, the temporal cycle, and the mandatory constraint.

payload.statement: The business shall maintain a record of each task available for completion.
payload.rationale: The Task entity represents work items that must be maintained as business artefacts at the owner level.

payload.statement: The business shall recognise child participants who complete tasks and earn money.
payload.rationale: The Child entity and Child role establish a business responsibility to recognise this participant category.

payload.statement: The business shall recognise parent participants who create tasks and make payments.
payload.rationale: The Parent entity establishes a business responsibility to recognise this participant category.

payload.statement: Each task shall have a completion status that distinguishes available tasks from completed tasks.
payload.rationale: The completion status attribute is a structural property of the Task entity required for business governance.

payload.statement: Each completed task shall be associated with the child participant who completed it.
payload.rationale: The task completion relationship is a structural assertion linking child entities to completed task entities.

payload.statement: The child shall be able to claim earned money upon task completion.
payload.rationale: The task claiming process defines a business capability that must be available to child participants.

payload.statement: The business shall account for each task completion event.
payload.rationale: The task completion event represents a business trigger that must be recorded and accounted for.

payload.statement: The business shall enable children to earn pocket money through task completion.
payload.rationale: The goal statement defines a core business capability to support child earning through work fulfillment.

payload.statement: The business shall maintain a record of each child's total income for each defined period.
payload.rationale: The weekly earnings aggregate entity represents an authoritative business artefact that must be maintained to support accountability and payment decisions.

payload.statement: The business shall record the association between each child and each task they have completed.
payload.rationale: The task completion relationship establishes a business responsibility to record child-task associations as the basis for earnings determination.

payload.statement: The business shall make available to parents aggregated data on child task completion and earnings.
payload.rationale: The earnings reporting function, parent role responsibility, and parent tracking goal together establish an obligation to provide parents with visibility into child income and work activity.

payload.statement: The business shall maintain a record of completed tasks beyond the current operational period.
payload.rationale: The historical task retention rule and the constraint on historical data persistence establish a governance obligation to maintain task records for accountability purposes.

payload.statement: The business shall recognise child participants as a distinct business role.
payload.rationale: WHAT-column entity and WHO-column role establish the business need to recognise children as a distinct participant class with unique responsibilities and entitlements.

payload.statement: The business shall make available to child participants visibility into their available earning opportunities.
payload.rationale: HOW-column task viewing function and WHY-column goal together establish the business capability to provide children access to their earning opportunities.

payload.statement: The business shall make available to child participants visibility into their completed work accomplishments.
payload.rationale: HOW-column task viewing function and WHY-column goal establish the business capability to provide children access to their accomplishments, distinct from available opportunities.

payload.title: High Level Description
payload.description: Section: High Level Description

payload.completeness_rule: This register SHALL contain the identifiers of ALL Segment elements present in the ledger.

payload.description: Core purpose statement defining the system's primary motivational goal to enable children to claim pocket money through task completion.
Parental control motivation establishing task definition and monetary reward assignment as a key purpose.
Child transparency objective expressing the motivational goal of visibility into available and completed tasks.
Accountability and reward visibility purpose enabling children to understand their weekly earnings.
Parental oversight and payment facilitation goal providing reporting to support pocket money distribution.
Document header indicating a conceptual overview of the system follows.
Core business concept describing the fundamental capability for children to claim pocket money tasks upon completion.
Business entity relationship between parents, tasks, and monetary amounts defining task creation capability.
Conceptual relationship establishing association between completed tasks and child entities.
Visibility capability requirement for child actors to view both available and completed task entities.
Weekly aggregation concept providing children visibility into total earnings as a derived business entity.
Parent-facing reporting capability concept aggregating child earnings and task completion data for payment purposes.
Temporal business rule defining weekly task availability lifecycle and reset behavior.
Data retention requirement ensuring historical task entity persistence beyond current operational period.

payload.description: Requirement for historical data could reflect motivational goals for trend analysis or merely technical data retention.
